title: Reversing Linked List, but with Strings

statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be a string, which represents a linked list of integers, where each character is a node in the list, and the integer value is the node's value.
statement.outputFormat: Return a new string representing a linked list where each value is the reverse of the input list's values.
statement.notes: The test cases may contain duplicate nodes, and there are no empty nodes in the list.

constraints.description: Length of the string in the input

examples.explanation: The input string is 12321. In the output string, we should reverse the value of each node. In this case, the output is the same: 12321.
Reverse the value of each node in the list

hints.text: Start by parsing the input string into an actual linked list, then reverse the values in the list and return the new list as a string.

editorial.title: Reversing Linked List, but with Strings
